content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5931 322-6441 322-6444

May 6, 1983

Raymond M. Haight
City Attorney
P.O. Box 66659
Scotts Valley, CA 95066

Re: Your Request for Written
Advice on Behalf of Mayor Rey
Retzlaff; Our File
No. A-83-104

Dear Mr. Haight:

Your letter asks for our advice regarding a decision
involving the Scotts Valley Drive Storm Drain Basin and
Improvement District. You have stated the facts as follows:

FACTS

1. Sometime on or about February 1982, the Scotts Valley
City Council adopted resolutions setting forth the proposed
boundaries of the above-referenced assessment district.

2. Should the assessment district proceed to completion,
public improvements that would be financed and constructed would
consist of such things as storm drains, curbs and gutters,
street lighting, and street widening.

3. Mayor Retzlaff does not own any property within the
boundaries of the proposed assessment district.

4. However, certain property commonly known as the "Hot Dog
Stand" property is owned by his children and their spouses.
None of the owners of the property reside within the City of
Scotts Valley.

5. Mayor Retzlaff's children (and their spouses) who own
the aforesaid property, collectively owe him a debt in excess of
$1,000. This debt is in the form of an unsecured note. Mayor
Retzlaff has no rights or interest in or to the "Hot Dog Stand"
property. The note requires no monthly payments, the sum owing
is merely due and payable at some date in the future.

Raymond M. Haight
May 6, 1983
Page 2


     6.  The property in question is worth something in excess of
$250,000.  It is currently rented to a tenant.  You do not know
the monthly rental amount, but believe it to be in excess of
$300 per month.

     7.  It appears that this property would be materially
affected by the proposed assessment district in that it would
undoubtedly bear substantial assessments once they are levied.

                         QUESTION PRESENTED

     The question presented for our review and advice is as
follows:

     On the basis of the foregoing fact situation, would Mayor
Retziaf have a conflict of interest with respect to
participating in decisions such as confirmation and spread of
levy of assessments, confirmation of engineer's report, approval
of design and construction plans, change of assessment district
boundaries, and the like?

                              ANALYSIS

     The Political Reform Act1/ requires that public officials
not participate in governmental decisions in which they have a
financial interest.  Section 87100.  Financial interest is
defined in Section 87103 to include certain investments, certain
interests in real property, sources of income and employes and
business entities where the official is an officer or director.

     In our previous advice letter (Advice Letter to Raymond M.
Haight, No. A-81-509, Oct. 16, 1981) we stated that Mr. Retziaf
had no interest in the real property in question since he had
sold it to his children and had taken in return only an
unsecured note.  However, that conclusion did not take into
account the provisions of Civil Code Section 3046, of which we
have since become apprised.  Civil Code Section 3046 provides
as follows:

     One who sells real property has a vendor's lien
     thereon, independent of possession, for so much of the
     price as remains unpaid and unsecured otherwise than by
     the personal obligation of the buyer.


_______________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91014.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise specified.

Raymond M. Haight
May 6, 1983
Page 3


The effect of this section is to continue in Mr. Retzlaff a
beneficial interest2/ in the "Hot Dog Stand" property in the
form of a vendor's lien. The statute creates a form of security
interest in the property where none is otherwise taken.

An interest in real property worth more than $1,000 can form
the basis of a conflict of interest.3/ Your letter indicates
that the "Hot Dog Stand" property will be materially affected by
the decisions. As we held previously,4/ effects upon it will
be distinguishable from effects on the public generally.
Therefore, Mr. Retzlaff has a conflict of interest as to the
storm drain basin decisions requiring his disqualification.

If you have any further questions regarding this letter,
please do not hesitate to contact me. I may be reached at
916/322-5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km


_________________________

2/ Section 82033 defines an "interest in real property"
as including any beneficial interest.

3/ Section 87103(b).

4/ Advice letter to Raymond M. Haight, No. A-81-509,
October 16, 1981, at pages 8-10.

RAYMOND M. HAIGHT
ATTORNEY AT LAW
4740 SCOTTS VALLEY DRIVE, SUITE A
P O BOX 66659
SCOTTS VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 95066
(408) 438-6610

April 11, 1983


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Request for Written Advice
Regarding Scotts Valley Drive Storm
Drain Basin and Improvement District -
Mayor Rey Retzlaff

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

Mayor Rey Retzlaff of the Scotts Valley City Council
has requested that I seek your written advice regarding the
following fact situation, involving the Scotts Valley Drive
Storm Drain Basin and Improvement District:

FACTS

1. Sometime on or about February, 1982, the Scotts
Valley City Council adopted resolutions setting forth the
proposed boundaries of the above-referenced assessment district.

2. Should the assessment district proceed to completion
public improvements that would be financed and constructed
would consist of such things as storm drains, curbs and gutters,
street lighting, and street widening.

3. Mayor Retzlaff does not own any property within
the boundaries of the proposed assessment district.

4. However, certain property commonly known as the
"Hot Dog Stand" property is owned by his children and their
spouses. None of the owners of the property reside within
the city of Scotts Valley.

5. Mayor Retzlaff's children (and their spouses)
who own the aforesaid property, collectively owe him a debt
in excess of $1,000.00. This debt is in the form of an un-
secured note. Mayor Retzlaff has no rights or interest in
or to the "Hot Dog Stand" property.

Robert E. Leídigh
April 11, 1983
PAGE TWO


     6.  The property in question is worth something in
excess of $250,000.00.  It is currently rented to a tenant.
I do not know the monthly rental amount, but I believe it
to be in excess of $300.00 per month.

     7.  It appears that this property would be materially
affected by the proposed assessment district in that it would
undoubtedly bear substantial assessments once they are levied.

                        QUESTION PRESENTED

     The question presented for your review and advice
is as follows:

          On the basis of the foregoing fact situation, would
     Mayor Retzlaff have a conflict of interest with respect to
     participating in decisions such as confirmation and spread
     of levy of assessments, confirmation of engineer's report,
     approval of design and construction plans, change of assess-
     ment district boundaries, and the like?

          If you need further clarification of the fact situation
     please contact me.

          Thank you in advance for your courtesy and cooperation
     in this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        ___________________________
                                        RAYMOND M. HAIGHT
RMH/pam                                 City Attorney

cc:  Mr. Bob Rocket,
     City Administrator

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901          322-6441             322-6444


April 19, 1983


Raymond M. Haight
Attorney at Law
P.O. BOX 66659
Scotts Valley, CA  95066

                                                Re: A-83-104

Dear Mr. Haight:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidig, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Does Mayor Retzlaff have a conflict of interest that would disqualify him from participating in decisions regarding a proposed assessment district when his children own property that would be materially affected by the district, and he holds an unsecured promissory note from them exceeding $1,000?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, Mayor Retzlaff has a conflict of interest and must be disqualified from participating in storm drain basin decisions. Although he does not directly own the affected property, California Civil Code Section 3046 creates a vendor's lien in his favor on the 'Hot Dog Stand' property, constituting a beneficial interest in real property exceeding $1,000 that will be materially affected by the assessment district decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (April 11, 1983) and FPPC response letter (May 6, 1983). Analysis section clearly discusses statutory basis (Section 87100, 87103, Civil Code 3046) and references prior advice letter A-81-509. Conclusion is explicit and unambiguous.

QUESTION: The question presented for our review and advice is as 
follows: 
On the basis of the foregoing fact situation, would Mayor 
Retzlaff have a conflict of interest with respect to 
participating in decisions such as confirmation and spread of 
levy of assessments, confirmation of engineer's report, approval 
of design and construction plans, change of assessment district 
boundaries, and the like?

CONCLUSION: Therefore, Mr. Retzlaff has a conflict of interest as to the storm drain basin decisions requiring his disqualification.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to City Attorney Haight's request regarding whether Mayor Rey Retzlaff has a conflict of interest in decisions affecting a proposed storm drain assessment district. The FPPC concludes that Retzlaff must recuse himself because his unsecured promissory note from his children creates a vendor's lien under Civil Code Section 3046, giving him a beneficial interest in their $250,000+ property that will be materially affected by the assessment district.
